title: Web Crawlers
description: Web crawlers use BFS to explore pages level by level from seed URLs, ensuring broad coverage before diving deep into any single site. A visited set (hash table) prevents re-crawling the same URL, and the BFS queue naturally prioritizes pages closer to the seed.

title: File System Traversal
description: The Unix 'find' command uses DFS to recursively walk directory trees. DFS is memory-efficient for deep hierarchies since it only keeps the current path on the stack, making it ideal for file systems that can have deeply nested directory structures.

title: Dependency Resolution
description: Package managers use DFS with cycle detection to resolve dependency graphs. Starting from the root package, DFS explores each dependency chain to its leaf, detecting circular dependencies via back-edges in the traversal and producing a valid installation order via post-order.
